description: Create a GitLab CI pipeline that uses the ubuntu:bionic Docker image and caches the APT package archives in an apt-cache directory keyed by "apt-cache". Define three stages—build, test, and deploy—and include a before_script that sets APT_CACHE_DIR, runs apt-get update, and installs qt5-qmake, qtbase5-dev, qtchooser, pkg-config, libqt5xmlpatterns5-dev, libsignon-qt5-dev, signon-plugins-dev, dbus-test-runner, lcov and gcovr using the cache directory. In the build_amd64 job (stage build) run export QT_SELECT=qt5, create a build folder, cd into it, execute qmake CONFIG+=debug CONFIG+=coverage .. and then make -j 4, and publish the whole repository as an artifact. In the test_amd64 job (stage test) which depends on build_amd64, cd into the build directory and run make coverage-html, also publishing all files as an artifact. Finally, add a pages job (stage deploy) depending on test_amd64 that creates a public folder, copies the generated coverage-html into public/coverage, and publishes the public directory as a GitLab Pages artifact.
